attacking_style: Does not attack traditionally; operates as diesel engine in breakaways, setting destructive tempo on climbs up to 16% gradient despite pure TT physiology. Maintains relentless pace rather than explosive accelerations.
team_tactics: Replicates Sky train methodology even in breakaway scenarios, riding sustained tempo for protected riders (Thomas). Executes leadout duties but ambiguous team hierarchy suggests near-winner capability creates role confusion.
terrain_preference: Dominates longer (14-26km) flat-to-rolling ITTs with technical sections where sustained power and bike handling combine. Surprisingly effective on moderate climbs when setting tempo rather than responding to attacks. Struggles on short (<10km) ITTs where explosive power trumps diesel advantage.
vulnerability: Grand Tour fatigue significantly degrades TT performance (40s losses to Van Aert). Climbing ITTs expose limits against pure GC contenders despite strong form. Short-distance ITTs neutralize sustained power advantage. Equipment issues/team conflicts emerge under pressure.
key_patterns: Beats Evenepoel on longer ITTs when fresh: 24s margin Tirreno, 16s Vuelta (2 instances)
Sets breakaway pace on steep gradients 14-16% despite 80kg+ physiology (2 instances)
Shows late-race sprint speed reserves beyond TT reputation, challenging for bunch kicks (2 instances)
Performance degrades 30-40 seconds in Grand Tour ITTs after 2+ weeks racing (2 instances)
Maintains 56kph averages on technical courses through superior power maintenance and handling (2 instances)
Effective at sustained chasing work after major climbing efforts, recovery anomaly for size (2 instances)
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta showed evolution beyond pure TT — setting breakaway pace on 16% climbs, challenging for sprint victories, beating Evenepoel. Expanding tactical repertoire while maintaining ITT dominance when fresh.
